description: Posture-aware routing suite for implementation-planning. The suite checks whether the skill behaves like a manual-first heavy planning protocol: it should activate only for explicit, complexity-justified technical planning requests and refuse adjacent asks such as brainstorming, clarification, business planning, or trivial execution planning.

assertion_definitions.routing_verdict_correct: The response makes the right top-level routing call: route to implementation-planning only for explicit, deep technical planning requests, and decline or redirect adjacent cases.
assertion_definitions.manual_first_boundary_respected: For routed cases, the response keeps the skill narrow and heavy rather than stretching it into general planning or execution.
assertion_definitions.complexity_gate_result_correct: The response correctly explains whether the task is complex enough for the heavy planning protocol.
assertion_definitions.adjacent_workflow_redirect_correct: For non-routed cases, the response points to the right neighboring workflow such as brainstorming, clarify, business-plan, or a lighter planner pass.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response closes with a concrete next step such as using the skill, staying with normal planning, or switching workflows first.

cases.name: Explicit risky migration request should trigger
cases.description: The task is a classic heavy-planning fit: explicit request, chosen direction, migration risk, and rollback needs.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'The planner already gathered context. Use implementation-planning to write the deep execution plan for our PostgreSQL-to-PostgreSQL schema migration with staged rollout and rollback checkpoints.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and what the next step is. Do not edit files.

manual_first_boundary_respected.check: The answer should keep the scope on deep technical planning rather than broadening to generic planning.

complexity_gate_result_correct.check: The answer should say the heavy planning protocol is justified.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the next step, such as drafting the deep plan with phases, verification, and rollback.

cases.name: Explicit cross-cutting refactor request should trigger
cases.description: The task spans multiple modules and interfaces, so a deep implementation plan is appropriate.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use implementation-planning for this feature-flag cleanup and service-boundary refactor. It touches the web app, API layer, background workers, and observability dashboards, and I want a real deep plan before any code changes.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and what the next step is. Do not edit files.

manual_first_boundary_respected.check: The answer should keep the skill positioned as a heavy planning protocol.

complexity_gate_result_correct.check: The answer should recognize the task as sufficiently complex and dependency-rich.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should say the next step is to draft the deep technical plan.

cases.name: Small isolated bug fix should not trigger
cases.description: The task is explicit but too small for the heavy protocol.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use implementation-planning for fixing a typo in one validation error string and updating the one snapshot test.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to implementation-planning.

complexity_gate_result_correct.check: The answer should explain that the task does not justify the heavy planning protocol.

adjacent_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should point to a lighter planner pass or direct execution workflow.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the lighter next step.

cases.name: Architecture option comparison should not trigger
cases.description: The request is still about deciding direction, so implementation-planning should refuse and redirect to brainstorming.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Help me decide whether we should keep the monolith or split to services, and then maybe give me a plan.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to implementation-planning yet.

adjacent_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to brainstorming or a decision-making workflow first.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should say what needs to happen before deep implementation planning makes sense.

cases.name: Contradictory constraints should not trigger yet
cases.description: The user asks for a plan, but the request is still contradictory and should go through clarify first.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use implementation-planning for redesigning our API, but keep total backward compatibility while removing all legacy fields immediately. I have not decided which clients must keep working.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to implementation-planning yet.

adjacent_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to clarify because the request still has unresolved contradictions.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should state the clarification step needed before planning.

cases.name: Business roadmap request should not trigger
cases.description: The wording includes planning and roadmap language, but the task is commercial rather than technical implementation planning.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Write a six-month product and go-to-market roadmap for our AI startup.' Using implementation-planning, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to implementation-planning.

adjacent_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to business-plan or another business planning workflow.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should point to the appropriate next workflow.

scoring.notes: Use this suite as a manual routing review after boundary or description changes. A case passes if it scores at least 75% of the possible points for the assertions that apply to that case.

runner_instructions.setup: Ensure the canonical implementation-planning package is discoverable. These prompts are intentionally routing-focused and should not require editing files.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt through a surface where the skill can activate. Capture whether the answer routes correctly and preserves the manual-first heavy boundary.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score each assertion as pass, partial, or fail after reading the captured response. Pay special attention to false positives on trivial, exploratory, or non-technical planning requests.
runner_instructions.environment: Filesystem write access is useful for workspace artifacts, but the prompts themselves should stay read-only.
